_meta.title: RRF G-Code Dictionary
_meta.description: This file contains parsed documentation for operators. Do not remove this _meta block.

+.title: Unary prefix/Binary infix operator
+.description:
### Unary

**Signatures**:
`int->int`
`float->float`

Unary +

`DateTime->int`

Converts a date & time value to a number of seconds since the datum. Available in RRF 3.4 and later.

### Binary

**Signatures**:
`(int,int)->int`
`(float,float)->float`
`(DateTime,int)->DateTime`

Addition. When adding an int to a DateTime the second operand is in seconds

-.title: Unary prefix/Binary infix operator
-.description:
### Unary

**Signatures**:
`int->int`
`float->float`

Unary -

### Binary

**Signatures**:
`(int,int)->int`
`(float,float)->float`
`(DateTime,DateTime)->int`
`(DateTime,int)->DateTime`

Subtraction. For DateTime values the result or operand is expressed in seconds.

#.title: Unary prefix operator
#.description:
**Signatures**: `X[]->int` `string->int`

Number of elements in array, or number of characters in string

*.title: Binary infix operator
*.description:
**Signatures**: `(int,int)->int` `(float,float)->float`

Multiplication

**Caution!** The multiplication operator * will work when it is used anywhere inside an expression or subexpression enclosed in { } but not otherwise. This is because the * character in a line of GCode normally introduces an end-of-line checksum.

!=.title: Binary infix operator
!=.description:
**Signature**: `(X,X)->bool`

Inequality (X stands for any type). Either the two operands must have the same type or one of them must be the constant **null**.

==.title: Binary infix operator
==.description:
**Signature**: `(X,X)->bool`

Equality (X stands for any type). Either the two operands must have the same type or one of them must be the constant `null`.

&&.title: Binary infix operator
&&.description:
**Signature**: `(bool,bool)->bool`

Logical AND.

*Note: In RRF, a single `&` can also be used.*

||.title: Binary infix operator
||.description:
**Signature**: `(bool,bool)->bool`

Logical OR.

*Note: In RRF, a single `|` can also be used.*
